name: Hank Jones
description: American jazz pianist, bandleader, arranger and composer (1918-2010)
abstract: Henry Jones Jr. (July 31, 1918 – May 16, 2010) was an American jazz pianist, bandleader, arranger, and composer.

quotes.text: Bud Johnson, tenor saxophone. Israel…not Chuck Israel…the bass player was in that band. Israel, that was his last name. Anyways, some of the players, let me think…Peanuts Huckle, clarinetist and saxophone player, Mousey Alexander, drummer. It’s hard to think of all these guys, you forget over time. It was a 15-piece band, it was a good band but not like some of the other bands that had… For instance, not like the band that headed Carnegie Hall. We had Gene Krupa.
quotes.caption: Hank Jones compares his band to Gene Krupa's Carnegie Hall band
quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: I did a CD with Hank Jones’s trio and a string quartet which I thought was just great. I mean Hank and I had been talking about doing something like that for years, and finally we got the opportunity to do it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Drummond
about_quotes.evidence: I have a tune, I don’t know if you’re familiar with a record of mine called “The Essence” on DMP with Hank Jones and Billy Higgins. A trio recording.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Drummond interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Norris Turney
about_quotes.evidence: Hank Jones was on piano, Seldon Powell on tenor, I played alto and clarinet. Richard Jones played trumpet. Nice little band, yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norris Turney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: Hank Jones was the piano player, Barry Gells was the guitarist, O.C. Johnson drums and yours truly bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Wess
about_quotes.evidence: In fact, we were in the Billy Ekstine band together in 1946... Hank was the pianist with us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Wess — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: I never played with Hank Jones. I hadn't met Hank Jones before and didn't know Ray Baretto.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
